title: Knowledge Graph (derived substrate artifact)
description: Derived typed graph over the Actian DS knowledge substrate. Nodes represent typed entities (components, categories, a11y criteria, foundation sections, motion patterns, content topics, apps, app entities, terminology terms, UX patterns); edges express typed relationships (in_category, a11y_ref, motion_ref, foundations_ref, related, narrower, in_app, entity_related, term_about). Regenerated by CI from the dist; consumers query for traversal (e.g. 'all a11y criteria that govern this component'). Do not edit by hand.

_schema_version.description: Schema version for the graph JSON shape. Monotonic integer; additive changes do not bump, breaking structural changes do. Consumers must accept any version >= the one they were built against and ignore unknown fields.

_meta.description: Auto-generation provenance marker. Signals that this file is derived — do not edit by hand.

examples.do_not_edit: Regenerated by CI from the dist; edit the source substrate, not this file.

generator.description: Relative path to the derive script that produced this file.

do_not_edit.description: Human-readable message reinforcing that this file is auto-generated.
examples: Regenerated by CI from the dist; edit the source substrate, not this file.

nodes.description: All typed entities in the graph. Each node has a namespaced id (<prefix>:<slug>), a type discriminator, and a human-readable title. Additional optional fields vary by type.

id.description: Namespaced node identifier: <type-prefix>:<slug>. The prefix dissolves cross-domain slug collisions (e.g. a11y:color-contrast vs foundation:color-contrast). Current prefixes: component, category, a11y, foundation, motion, content, app, entity, term, pattern.

type.description: Node type discriminator. Determines which prefix is used in id and which edge types are meaningful.

title.description: Human-readable node title as it appears in the substrate.

wcag.description: WCAG success-criterion numbers (e.g. '1.4.3') for a11y_criterion nodes.

provenance.description: Provenance marker for derived/structural nodes — e.g. 'figma-dskit' on category nodes (Figma-derived grouping, not team-canonical).

description.description: Human-readable description/purpose for app-context nodes (app.purpose, app_entity.description, ux_pattern.description).
examples: Data governance, catalog management, stewardship, lineage

definition.description: SKOS-style definition for terminology_term nodes: the canonical meaning of the term.

hiddenLabels.description: SKOS hidden labels for terminology_term nodes: the discouraged / not-to-use alternatives for the preferred term.

figmaKey.description: Stable Figma component key (rename-proof) carried as a queryable external identifier on component nodes. From the registry entry; slug remains the node id.

figmaNodeId.description: Figma node id of the component's set-level frame, carried beside figmaKey.

edges.description: Typed directed relationships between nodes. Sorted deterministically (by type then source then target) for stable diffs.

source.description: Namespaced id of the source node.

target.description: Namespaced id of the target node.

type.description: Edge type discriminator. in_category: component→category membership. a11y_ref/motion_ref/foundations_ref: P8 transversal taxonomy cross-references. related: non-hierarchical association. narrower: SKOS-style broader/narrower hierarchy (foundation_section→foundation_section; non-transitive). in_app: app_entity/ux_pattern→app membership. entity_related: app_entity→app_entity (specific relation name in the predicate field). term_about: terminology_term→app_entity/app/ux_pattern (inferred term-to-node bridge).

note.description: Optional human-readable annotation on this edge (e.g. context for why the ref applies).
examples: applies to all interactive surfaces in this component

rationale.description: Optional longer-form rationale for the relationship, when the source provides it.
examples: WCAG 2.4.3 requires focus order to be logical for all interactive elements.

scope.description: For transversal-ref edges: whether the ref was declared at the category level (inherited by members) or the component level (specific).

confidence.description: Trust level of the relationship. Deterministically derived refs are 'asserted'.

predicate.description: For entity_related edges: the authored relationship name from the app-context entity model (e.g. hasInputPorts, belongsTo). Keeps the controlled edge vocabulary small while preserving the specific relation.

provenance.description: Where this edge came from (PROV-style: source file, deriver, extraction method).

source_file.description: Authored source file the edge was derived from.
